paper_map.title: Microstructure and mechanical properties of a Fe–28Mn–9Al-1.2C-(0, 3, 6, 9)Cr austenitic low-density steel
paper_map.abstract_summary: The effects of Cr addition (0-9 wt%) on the microstructure, carbide formation, and mechanical properties of Fe–28Mn–9Al-1.2C strips produced by near-rapid solidification are investigated. Cr addition leads to a nonlinear yield strength evolution in as-cast state—firstly decreasing then increasing beyond the Cr-free strip—while total elongation clearly drops, associated with reduced κ-carbide precipitation, increased dislocation density, and formation of Cr23C6 in the 9Cr strip. Annealing at 550 °C for 5 h enhances yield strengths via additional carbide precipitation, but while the Cr-free strip loses ductility after annealing, all Cr-containing strips gain ductility due to dislocation reduction. Cr23C6 at austenite grain boundaries is responsible for the lowest ductility in 9Cr material; both slip bands and dislocations contribute to ductility in 0Cr and 6Cr strips.
paper_map.research_objective: To systematically clarify the influence of Cr content (0-9 wt%) on microstructure, carbide evolution and mechanical properties of high-Mn high-C Fe–28Mn–9Al-1.2C austenitic low-density steel processed by near-rapid solidification.
main_process_variables: Near-rapid solidification (centrifugal casting into copper mold, rotating speed 600 r/min, maximum cooling rate ~5×10³ K/s, strip thickness 2.5 mm)
Annealing: 550 °C for 5 h in vacuum electric resistance furnace
expected_information_axis: microstructure: phase constituents, κ-carbide and Cr23C6 precipitation, dislocation density, slip bands, grain boundaries
property: yield strength, total elongation, density
mechanism: dislocation evolution, precipitation strengthening, grain boundary embrittlement by Cr23C6, deformation mechanisms (slip bands, dislocations)
figure_evidence: XRD patterns, optical/transmission electron micrographs, tensile curves
key_sections: 1. Introduction: background on low-density steels, role of κ-carbides and Cr addition, motivation for studying high-Mn high-C variant
2. Experimental: alloy preparation, centrifugal casting, annealing, characterization methods (OM, TEM, XRD, tensile tests)
3.1 Microstructure and mechanical properties of as-cast strips
3.2 Microstructure and mechanical properties of strips after annealing
4. Discussion: interpretation of carbide evolution, dislocation density effects, ductility trends
